diagrams.title: File Allocation Methods Comparison
diagrams.description: Shows three allocation methods: contiguous (blocks 5,6,7 together), linked (blocks connected via pointers), indexed (index block points to data blocks)
diagrams.purpose: Understanding how files are physically stored on disk and their structural differences

diagrams.title: UNIX inode Structure
diagrams.description: Inode structure showing 12 direct pointers, single indirect, double indirect, and triple indirect pointers leading to data blocks
diagrams.purpose: Understanding how UNIX manages files of different sizes using multi-level indexing

diagrams.title: Free Space Management - Bit Vector
diagrams.description: Bitmap representation of disk blocks where 0=free and 1=allocated, showing efficient free space tracking
diagrams.purpose: Visualizing how bit vectors track free disk space efficiently

diagrams.title: File System Architecture Layers
diagrams.description: Shows layers from user commands to physical disk: Application → System calls → File System Interface → File System Implementation → Device Driver → Disk
diagrams.purpose: Understanding the layered architecture of file system implementation
